requirements: 3U CubeSat primary frame, Al 7075, PC/104 standoffs, deployable rail guide grooves (CDS spec)
Structural frame for 3U CubeSat with deployable rail guides and PC/104 board standoffs
Supports avionics, power, and payload modules
Thermal range: -120°C to +120°C
Launch vibration load: 20g
Material: Al 7075, CFRP, FR4
Process: CNC machining
Dimensions: 100x100x340mm

risk.mit: Verify electrical connectivity through harness passthroughs during testing.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: 3U CubeSat primary frame, Al 7075, PC/104 standoffs, deployable rail guide grooves (CDS spec)

physics_paths.description: Structural loads from launch vibration are transferred through the frame to the mounting points of internal components.
Thermal gradients are managed by the frame's design to ensure uniform temperature distribution.
Deployable rail guides allow for the deployment of external payloads.

integration_opportunities.description: Thermal management improvements can be made by adding heat sinks or thermal vias to the frame.
Structural integrity can be enhanced by reinforcing critical load paths with additional ribs or stiffeners.

verification_focus.description: Structural integrity under launch loads and thermal cycling should be verified.
Thermal performance under specified temperature ranges should be verified.
Electrical connectivity through harness passthroughs should be verified.

risk.desc: Thermal cycling may cause unexpected deformation in the spring-loaded hinge.
risk.mit: Include thermal expansion joints and conduct thermal analysis.

risk.mit: Review and adjust clearance dimensions to ensure proper fit.

risk.desc: Inadequate locking evidence for fasteners may lead to loosening in space.
risk.mit: Ensure visible locking evidence and preload intent for all critical joints.
